summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on regulation, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: Block inches closer to DOJ settlement
articles.description: The company set aside $240 million in the first quarter as it negotiates with the Justice Department over allegations in a 2023 short seller’s report.

articles.title: Shopify loses bid to toss Sezzle lawsuit
articles.description: The e-commerce platform can't jettison plausible monopoly claims made by the buy now, pay later company, a federal judge ruled Monday.

articles.title: PayPal settles with DOJ over DEI
articles.description: The digital payments company agreed Tuesday to a settlement with the Department of Justice over a 2020 diversity investment fund.

articles.title: FIS discounts Pismo competition
articles.description: The payments processor and core tech provider doesn’t expect competition from that Visa business to make an impact.

articles.title: Judge combines Fiserv lawsuits
articles.description: Shareholder complaints against the payment processor are closely related and should be adjudicated in a New York case, a Wisconsin federal judge ruled.

articles.title: Senators query credit bureaus on BNPL
articles.description: A group of Democrats wants to know how credit agencies are using buy now, pay later data and what changes may be planned in the future.

articles.title: North and Visa team to elevate the payments game
articles.description: North and Visa, two of the biggest names in fintech, have partnered to deliver next-level solutions that meet the demands of today's complex payments landscape.

articles.title: Appeals court remands Illinois card fee case
articles.description: A move by federal officials to preempt Illinois’ interchange fee ban requires further review by a district court, the 7th Circuit said Friday.
